name: Infused Creatures
description: Global entry for Grounded's New Game+ infused creatures. It is not one species: it summarizes the infusion system that alters existing enemies with new properties.
behavior: An infused creature keeps much of its original species identity, but gains extra layers of power, elemental anomalies, or effects that can radically change the real danger of the encounter.
interactionWithPlayer: It should be read as an NG+ system warning. The base page still matters, but it is no longer enough on its own: infusion can make a formerly manageable creature lethal.
interactionWithCreatures: It does not define a fixed relationship between species: each infused enemy inherits its base species and then layers on properties that can harden damage, space control, or elemental pressure.
strategy: The general rule is to approach with far more respect, re-check the real weaknesses and resistances, and assume that a familiar moveset may now carry new punishments. In NG+, the infusion matters almost as much as the base species.

loot.item: Check the base species and the active infusion.

combatStats.attackDamageSummary: Varies by creature and active infusion. The same enemy can change dramatically between cycles or property combinations.

abilities.name: Infused Property
abilities.description: Each infused creature gains one or more added properties that modify its damage, zone control, or the way it punishes mistakes.

abilities.name: NG+ Scaling
abilities.description: On top of the base moveset, the threat rises through New Game+ scaling, so even familiar species can erase mistakes far more easily.

abilities.name: Anomalous Combinations
abilities.description: Infusion can combine damage or effects you would not expect from the base version, so it is worth re-reading resistances, weaknesses, and elemental pressure before getting comfortable.

specialTraits: This is not a single creature: it summarizes the NG+ infused system.
A manageable base creature can become extreme if the right infusion is layered onto it.
lesserMutationsDescription: Infused creatures can gain extra random lesser mutations. Possible outcomes include explosions, candy magic, summons, creature duplication, life steal, and temporary attack or damage boosts, among other alterations.
lesserMutations: Extra explosions tied to attacks or close contact.
Candy magic that adds unexpected elemental pressure.
Summoned helper creatures joining the fight.
Creature duplication or a split into two enemies.
Life steal that extends bad trades.
Attack boosts that punish mistakes much harder.
Damage boosts or other random alterations compatible with the system.
